name: source_dataset
description: Canonical short source name. 'infys' = Mexican volume workbook (INFYS / SNIF national forest inventory). 'dina' = mangrove biomass CSV.

name: source_record_id
description: Per-source stable id minted at ingest time. Format: 'infys_NNNNN' or 'dina_NNN'.

name: state
description: Mexican state name (volume rows only). Null for biomass rows.

name: umafor_code
description: UMAFOR (Unidad de Manejo Forestal Regional) code, e.g. '0101'. STRING is required to preserve leading zeros; loading as INT64 would corrupt 4486 rows in the current snapshot.

name: mx_inegi_cveecon4
description: INEGI / CONABIO Clave Ecorregion Nivel 4 (Mexican-domain proper-noun identifier; Spanish-origin code retained intentionally).

name: scientific_name_apg_raw
description: Species scientific name as it appears in the source. APG nomenclature for volume rows; common species names for biomass rows.

name: scientific_name_standardized
description: Standardized scientific name (currently equals raw for biomass rows; null for volume rows -- no taxonomic standardization applied yet).

name: assignment_level
description: Volume-source assignment level. Documented values 1-8; 9, 10, 11 also appear in the data and have no description in the source workbook (see 05_governance/risks.md). Null for biomass rows.

name: assignment_level_desc
description: Spanish description of the assignment level, denormalized inline. Null when assignment_level has no source-side description (values 9-11 in the current snapshot) or for biomass rows.

name: equation_code
description: Source-side equation identifier (volume only).

name: source_code
description: Bibliographic source key (volume only). Two distinct values in the current snapshot: '1erINF61-85', 'Siplafor'.

name: source_reference
description: Bibliographic reference text, denormalized inline (volume only).

name: equation_raw
description: Original equation text from source. Predictor tokens may be Spanish-derived (e.g. 'Diam', 'Altura', 'DAP', 'WD'); see README.md 'Source-language note'.

name: equation_normalized
description: Lowercased, token-standardized equation. Predictor tokens are diam (DBH cm), alt (height m), wd (wood density tonne/m3).

name: equation_numpy
description: NumPy-evaluable RHS expression. Volume uses np.exp / np.log / np.power; biomass uses Python ** for exponentiation. Suitable for `eval(expr, {'np': np}, {'diam': ..., 'alt': ..., 'wd': ...})`.

name: equation_numpy_wd_fixed
description: Biomass-only: equation_numpy with wd already substituted to the species' literal wood-density value (e.g. '0.403*0.78*(diam)**1.934' for Avicennia germinans). Null for volume rows.

name: dbh_range_cm_raw
description: Original DBH applicability range string from the source workbook (volume only).

name: dbh_min_cm
description: Parsed minimum DBH in centimetres.

name: dbh_max_cm
description: Parsed maximum DBH in centimetres.

name: height_range_m_raw
description: Original total-tree-height applicability range string from the source workbook (volume only).

name: height_min_m
description: Parsed minimum total tree height in metres.

name: height_max_m
description: Parsed maximum total tree height in metres.

name: wood_density_value
description: Wood density (biomass rows only). Units in wood_density_units.

name: wood_density_units
description: Units of wood_density_value, e.g. 'tonne/m3'.

name: wood_density_source
description: Filename of the wood-density reference used for the join (e.g. 'wood_density_values_table29.csv').

name: response_variable
description: Response variable symbol. 'VRTAcc' for volume rows, 'B' for biomass rows.

name: response_units
description: Response units. 'm3' for volume rows, 'kg' for biomass rows.

name: parse_status
description: Parsing outcome. 'ok' or 'missing_equation'. Currently 0 rows are 'missing_equation' in the live snapshot.

name: parse_notes
description: Free-text parsing notes; usually null. STRING type matters: a bare CSV-to-parquet pass would coerce this all-null column to LOGICAL and break a STRING load (regression caught and fixed in R/07_export_parquet_unified.R).
